persona_json.name: Maya — The Scrappy Brand Builder
persona_json.summary: Maya is a hands-on creative professional—either a freelancer, small agency owner, or in-house brand strategist—who works directly with clients and needs tight brand control. She uses design tools daily to build, export, and share branded assets, and views project management software primarily as a vehicle for managing brand consistency and client deliverables.
goals: Build and maintain a consistent brand kit that scales across multiple client projects
Share finalized branded assets with clients quickly and professionally
Export assets in multiple formats to meet different channel and client requirements
Experiment with typography and color palettes to find the perfect brand voice
Reduce friction between design decisions and client handoff
pains: Managing brand consistency across projects when working with multiple clients or team members
Time spent manually adjusting assets for different platforms instead of templating
Difficulty sharing work-in-progress or final assets with clients who aren't designers
Lack of pre-built templates to accelerate initial brand direction exploration
Friction in handing off assets to clients in the format they actually need
motivations: Wants to look professional to clients and prove attention to detail
Driven by urgency—clients expect turnaround quickly, so tools must be fast
Motivated to own the entire creative process from conception to delivery
Wants to differentiate their brand work through custom typography and color choices
Seeks validation that their design decisions are working by testing them in live templates
objections: Will reject tools that slow down the design-to-export workflow
Skeptical of overly rigid or opinionated design systems that limit customization
Concerned about learning curve—needs tools that are intuitive for solo or small team use
Won't pay for enterprise features they don't need as a 1-5 person operation
channels: Design collaboration tools (Figma, Adobe Creative Cloud)
Marketing and branding communities (Design Twitter, Brand Designers Slack groups)
Template marketplaces and design inspiration sites
decision_triggers: Sees peer in their community using a tool that speeds up their workflow by 50%
Gets urgent client request that exposes a gap in their current toolkit
Free trial or freemium tier that lets them test without commitment
Case study or testimonial from another freelancer or small agency doing similar work
Tool integrates natively with their existing design stack (Figma, Adobe, etc.)
sample_quotes: I need to export these assets in five different formats by EOD—the tool has to keep up with me, not slow me down.
When I share a brand kit with a client, I want them to see exactly how their logo looks in context, not just in isolation.
Templates are great for getting started, but they can't be the final answer—I have to be able to customize everything.
My clients are paying for my eye and my decisions. I'm not letting a tool override my creative judgment.

journey_stages.mindset: Recognizes that ad-hoc asset management and repetitive exports are eating into billable time
key_actions: Browsing templates to understand how design systems could work for them
Testing color and font tools to see if they unlock new workflow possibilities

journey_stages.mindset: Evaluating whether a brand kit tool would truly save time or become just another platform to manage
key_actions: Uploading custom fonts and exporting in multiple formats to stress-test the tool
Sharing client-ready assets to see how the workflow feels
content_preferences: How-to guides for brand kit creation
Comparisons of export formats and their use cases
Real freelancer reviews and success stories

journey_stages.mindset: Ready to commit if the tool demonstrably cuts export-to-handoff time and handles their specific file format needs
key_actions: Setting up a complete brand kit for a real or test project
Inviting a client to review assets in the tool
Checking integration points with their design software
content_preferences: Pricing comparisons tailored to small teams
Customer success stories with measurable time savings

beliefs: Brand consistency is a competitive advantage that justifies the upfront investment in kit-building
Clients trust designers more when they can see and comment on work in real-time
Typography and color are the fastest ways to communicate brand personality
Pre-built templates can accelerate initial ideation if they're customizable enough
values: Hands-on ownership: wants full control over creative output, not abstraction or delegation
Speed to market: values tools that compress iteration cycles, especially for client-facing work
Transparency in delivery: believes clients should always see what they're getting before final handoff
Craft-driven: prefers intentional, thoughtful design decisions over templates or automation that oversimplifies
